steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the structure of the passage: it opens with a specific historical example (Jennie Carter's 1867 journalism), then describes an organization that preserves and honors that history (the NABJ).

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A: misidentifies the passage's content or reverses the order of the two parts. ✗
  • Choice B — specific achievement in Black journalistic history, then an organization dedicated to upholding that history: matches exactly — Carter's 1867 work is the specific achievement; the NABJ, founded to honor people like Carter, is the organization. ✓
  • Choice C: introduces unsupported elements not present in the passage. ✗
  • Choice D: describes a different structural relationship than what the passage presents. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
